name: queue-small-team-ordering
decision: Queue between order service and email sender. Constraints: per-user ordering matters, at-least-once is fine, ~1,000 msg/s, small team with limited ops capacity, already all-in on AWS serverless.
note: Kafka/MSK is a poor fit for a small team with limited ops capacity — high operational burden.

name: datastore-cost-constrained
decision: Primary datastore for a simple key-value session store. Hard constraint: keep monthly cost minimal, tiny team, spiky low traffic, serverless-first. Simple access patterns (get/put by key).
note: For minimal cost + tiny team + spiky low traffic + serverless, DynamoDB on-demand fits; the others carry ops burden / idle cost.

name: sync-vs-async-fanout
decision: When an order is placed we must notify up to millions of followers. Constraint: the user's request must return fast; delivery can be eventually consistent.
options: Synchronous fan-out in the request handler
Publish one event to a queue, workers fan out asynchronously
must_not_recommend: Synchronous fan-out in the request handler
note: Synchronous fan-out in the request path is clearly wrong when the request must return fast.
